title: butomissa tatarica
intro: Butomissa tatarica, also known as the flowering water-plantain, is a perennial aquatic plant that belongs to the Butomaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral activities. Butomissa tatarica can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and has also been used in traditional folk medicine as a poultice to treat skin conditions.
